data.acceptance:
1. GET /api/approvals returns canonical approval escalation records independently of GET /api/terminal and includes approvalId, notificationKey, status, createdAt, updatedAt, repoPath, beatId, terminalSessionId, agent provider/name/model/version, adapter/source, toolName, normalized toolCall, patterns, supportedActions, and actionable state.
2. GET /api/approvals supports polling-friendly filters for repo, status or active-only, and updatedSince or an equivalent cursor, with deterministic ordering.
3. Pending/unresolved approvals remain visible through GET /api/approvals after terminal auto-close, backend session cleanup, or removal from GET /api/terminal, until an explicit approve, always_approve, reject, manual-action, or dismiss transition.
4. POST /api/approvals/{approvalId}/actions accepts approve, always_approve, and reject, delegates to the active runtime responder when available, updates the canonical approval record, and returns the resulting status.
5. If an approval is no longer programmatically actionable because the session/responder is gone, the action API returns a deterministic 409 or 410 error, records that state, and keeps the approval visible for manual handling.
6. The existing POST /api/terminal/{sessionId}/approvals/{approvalId} route remains compatible or delegates to the new canonical approval action implementation.
7. The UI approval hydration path uses the canonical backend approval list so the Approvals tab, bell badge, banner, toast, and direct API pollers observe the same queue.
8. /api/openapi.json documents the approval list/action routes, ApprovalEscalation record schema, action request/response schemas, filters, and terminal-session pendingApprovals compatibility field.
9. Automated coverage proves listing, filtering, action success, unavailable-responder failure, session-cleanup persistence, UI hydration from the approval API, and OpenAPI schema coverage.
10. Focused approval API tests, bun run lint, bunx tsc --noEmit, bun run test, and bun run build pass.
